topic: Best strategy to enforce autonomous-research-loop stopping only after proof found or max iterations exhausted
scope: Strategy discussion only; no code edits by subagents
constraints: Use repo evidence summarized by parent

agreement: Runtime helper must enforce hard iteration-budget invariants
Validation must fail semantically impossible ledgers
No OK/running status when remaining iterations are zero
Final continue should be rejected or converted to terminal budget exhaustion; most roles prefer rejection
round_1_summary.main_tension: How much success/proof evidence validation belongs in this first patch versus a later schema upgrade

consensus: Patch 1 should be a narrow runtime enforcement fix for budget and running-state invariants
Do not bundle proof/success evidence schema into Patch 1
Reject final or over-budget continue decisions instead of silently converting them
Validation should fail over-budget, desynchronized, or zero-remaining running ledgers
Patch 2 should add machine-checkable stop reasons and proof/success evidence
round_2_summary.best_strategy: Make the runtime helper the authoritative enforcement boundary for ledger arithmetic and terminality first, then extend the schema for success/proof evidence once the immediate loop-control bug is fixed.
